Kilimall Preferred Store — Active
2014
Completed KCSE at Maryland Mountain Academy with C+. Received civil engineering offer from TUM — unable to enrol due to lack of guidance and family means.
2014 – 2015
Cleaning services at Kenyatta University. Security officer at Brinks, guarding Chancellors Tower. Recording meetings on his phone to study logistics at night.
2016
Joined Kilimall as a casual picker. Became best picker → promoted to Picking Team Leader → promoted to Logistics Supervisor over diploma-holders.
2018
Hand-picked by CEO in a boardroom he was never meant to enter. Became a pioneer of KiliShops — a national pickup-point network that saved the company millions.
2020 – Present
Supply Operations at Glovo. 5+ years mastering real-time logistics, fleet management, and crisis operations across a tripling courier network.
Now
Titled landowner. E-commerce operator. Precision farmer. Building a sovereign estate for his son's inheritance.
The story

Built from
nothing owned

"I watched my former classmates walk into university while I reported for my security shift. I chose to become dangerous with knowledge instead."
Nairobi · 2014–2015
Brinks Security · Kenyatta University

From the archive — before the empire.

No family guidance. No HELB loan. No safety net. Peter Kuria entered the workforce at 19 with a C+ and a refusal to accept that a certificate defines capability. He cleaned floors at the university he should have attended, guarded the library filled with books he taught himself from, and recorded boardroom meetings on his phone because no one would teach him formally.

At Kilimall, he outperformed diploma-holders through sheer precision and data instinct — rising from casual picker to Logistics Supervisor and eventually pioneering KiliShops, a national infrastructure project that transformed last-mile delivery economics in Kenya.

At Glovo, he mastered real-time operations and supply chain management across one of East Africa's fastest-growing delivery networks. Today, that corporate expertise funds a parallel empire: two titled land parcels, a livestock breeding program, a Preferred e-commerce store, and a 3-bedroom sanctuary under development for his son.

Peter is not transitioning out of logistics. He is using logistics to fund a sovereign life — and documenting every step.

Precision Farmer

Dorper sheep as a mobile bank. H6213 maize as seasonal capital. Bees, rabbits, and doves completing a closed-loop ecosystem.

Agriculture

Data Strategist

Alison-certified in MySQL and statistical analysis. Bridging corporate supply chains with rural prosperity through data-driven decisions.

A data-driven drop-shipping operation running at Preferred status — Kilimall's mark of excellence for consistent order accuracy and customer satisfaction. Every sale funds physical infrastructure: a mousepad sold today is a concrete pillar planted tomorrow.

A closed-loop precision agriculture system under active development. Dorper sheep serve as a living savings account. H6213 maize delivers seasonal capital. Water independence through a 40ft+ deep well secures the entire system against drought. Bees and rabbits complete a high-efficiency food forest.
